jokes.text: Perhaps a gun.
jokes.setup: Offered another aspirin for his headache
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey casually suggests a gun as alternative medicine
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and economic - two words that completely subvert the setup with perfect Geoffrey deadpan

jokes.text: When Crackle and Pop cut a disc, then call me.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions the band 'Snap' is great
jokes.punchline: Philip extends the cereal reference to Rice Krispies mascots
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay joke that shows Philip's wit while dismissing youth culture

jokes.text: What's so funny? - Carlton told a joke. No, Hilary. Carlton is a joke.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks what's funny about Carlton telling a joke
jokes.punchline: Will's brutal correction that Carlton himself is the joke
jokes.explanation: Classic Will burn on Carlton with perfect misdirection and economy

jokes.text: Didn't you have a schedule for after school at my age? Yeah, the TV Guide.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks about Will's after-school schedule as a kid
jokes.punchline: Will's working-class reality - TV Guide was his only schedule
jokes.explanation: Great class contrast joke that defines Will's character perfectly - relatable and specific

jokes.text: I just pictured it.
jokes.setup: Question about Ashley showing up to violin in tennis outfit
jokes.punchline: Philip's pause as he visualizes this absurd image
jokes.explanation: Simple visual comedy beat - audience imagines the same absurd scene

jokes.text: I'm two for two. I'm going to quit while I'm ahead.
jokes.setup: Philip has made two successful jokes
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges his rare comedic success and wants to preserve it
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about Philip's usually poor joke-telling - self-aware character moment

jokes.text: May I suggest retirement?
jokes.setup: Philip wants to quit while ahead with his jokes
jokes.punchline: Will suggests he retire from joke-telling entirely
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up burn but predictable - Will can't resist taking Philip down a peg

jokes.text: I just don't want to do it, okay?
jokes.setup: Hilary's elaborate excuse about pollution and carpools
jokes.punchline: She admits the real reason with childish petulance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her selfishness breaking through fake concern

jokes.text: Word up. Kick over the keys to the Jag. - The Benzito? - The station wagon.
jokes.setup: Will eagerly expects to drive luxury cars
jokes.punchline: Reality check - he gets the family station wagon
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Will's street slang confidence to suburban reality

jokes.text: Come on, you're gonna ruin my rep. You're only 17. You don't have a rep yet.
jokes.setup: Will worried about his reputation
jokes.punchline: Philip's matter-of-fact dismissal of teenage concerns
jokes.explanation: Standard parent-teen dynamic joke with decent delivery

jokes.text: The same thing I learned from banging my head against a pole: Don't do it again.
jokes.setup: Vivian suggests he might learn something from violin
jokes.punchline: Will's analogy comparing violin to self-injury
jokes.explanation: Solid analogy joke showing Will's street-smart perspective on high culture

jokes.text: I'll be more than happy to drive you to the Piggly Wiggly, Miss Daisy.
jokes.setup: Will accepts his chauffeur duties reluctantly
jokes.punchline: Reference to 'Driving Miss Daisy' with grocery store substitution
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference joke that works for Will's character but not particularly original

jokes.text: You make me kind of nervous with that stick, lady.
jokes.setup: Intimidating violin teacher confronts Will
jokes.punchline: Will's honest reaction to her conductor's baton
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about the violin teacher's intensity

jokes.text: I wasn't as tight with the man as you were.
jokes.setup: Teacher scolds him for not respecting Stravinsky
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic response about not knowing Stravinsky personally
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that shows Will's wit against pretension

jokes.text: Do not attempt to make joke with me. I was lover of both Laurel and Hardy.
jokes.setup: Teacher warns Will not to joke with her
jokes.punchline: She claims to have been romantically involved with both comedy partners
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd line that perfectly captures the character's pretentious delusions

jokes.text: Hey, Ash, that was decent to listen to that kind of music... without having to see Freddy Krueger kill somebody.
jokes.setup: Will compliments Ashley's violin playing
jokes.punchline: Compares classical music favorably to horror movie soundtracks
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's cultural perspective but joke is a bit forced

jokes.text: No, wait. That's tennis.
jokes.setup: Ashley says violin will be useful when she's older
jokes.punchline: She realizes she's thinking of tennis, not violin
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection showing Ashley's confusion about adult expectations

jokes.text: Ash, the world does not need another Philip Banks. We might not have enough farmland to support the one we have.
jokes.setup: Will argues Ashley doesn't need to follow her father's path
jokes.punchline: Implies Philip is so large they need agricultural resources to sustain him
jokes.explanation: Creative fat joke that ties into the larger theme while roasting Philip

jokes.text: Let's just say that lady expects a lot from a piece of wood and some strings.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks what the teacher meant about violin fulfilling needs
jokes.punchline: Will's euphemistic explanation of the teacher's romantic metaphors
jokes.explanation: Smart callback to teacher's weird romantic violin talk with subtle innuendo

jokes.text: And, Geoffrey, they were just repainting the office.
jokes.setup: Philip lists increasingly loud construction noises
jokes.punchline: Reveals they were only repainting, making the noise absurd
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with effective punchline that reframes everything

jokes.text: Then what was the point of the story? Sheer entertainment, sir.
jokes.setup: Philip asks for the point of Geoffrey's pointless story
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey admits it was purely for his own amusement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Geoffrey moment - his deadpan admission of wasting Philip's time for fun

jokes.text: I believe the technical term is a 'rim shot.'
jokes.setup: Loud drumming sound from upstairs
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey provides unnecessarily technical explanation
jokes.explanation: Typical Geoffrey over-formality applied to obvious situation

jokes.text: He owns this great store where you don't need any money. You just go in and give him something you don't even want anymore... and he gives you this ticket.
jokes.setup: Ashley explains where her violin went
jokes.punchline: Her innocent description of how a pawnshop works
jokes.explanation: Child's innocent perspective on adult world creates gentle humor

jokes.text: You don't have many pawnshops in Bel-Air. That's really a shame.
jokes.setup: Philip's shock about the pawnshop visit
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically lamenting the lack of pawnshops in wealthy area
jokes.explanation: Good class-contrast joke showing Will's street perspective on Bel-Air

jokes.text: Daddy, it's a diamond. No, it isn't. - You don't like it? - No, I do not. Can I have it?
jokes.setup: Ashley shows off her 'diamond' from the pawnshop
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately asks for it when Philip rejects it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her materialism overrides any other consideration

jokes.text: A girlie that can play the drums can write her own ticket.
jokes.setup: Philip's speech about accomplished young violin ladies
jokes.punchline: Will's street-smart counter about drums opening doors
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's different values but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Philip, when I met you, you were into James Brown. - He liked James Brown? - He even wore his hair like him. He had hair?
jokes.setup: Vivian reveals Philip's funky past
jokes.punchline: Carlton's shock that his father ever had hair
jokes.explanation: Good escalating revelation about Philip's past with Carlton's naive reaction

jokes.text: You got that right. Man, you're loaded!
jokes.setup: Philip introduces himself formally
jokes.punchline: Jazz immediately comments on the family's wealth
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jazz's character as direct and impressed by wealth

jokes.text: From now on, I'm no longer Philip Banks. Just call me King Edward IV. As you wish, Your Majesty.
jokes.setup: Philip sarcastically adopts royal name to mock kids' nicknames
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately plays along with mock formality
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay with Geoffrey enabling Philip's sarcasm

jokes.text: Humor, sir.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey plays along with Philip's royal persona
jokes.punchline: He flatly explains it was just humor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan - explaining the obvious with formal detachment

jokes.text: I can't answer that question for you, son. Ask your mother.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Christmas will be ruined next
jokes.punchline: Philip deflects to Vivian with domestic cowardice
jokes.explanation: Standard henpecked husband joke but fits Philip's character

jokes.text: Same thing.
jokes.setup: Philip says he wants Ashley to spend time with family, not punishment
jokes.punchline: Hilary equates family time with punishment
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary self-centeredness with perfect economy

jokes.text: but she will not be on Yo! MTVRaps if you all don't work with me.
jokes.setup: Jazz talks about Ashley's potential music career
jokes.punchline: His unrealistic vision of Ashley on hip-hop TV show
jokes.explanation: Absurd ambition from Jazz showing his disconnection from reality

jokes.text: Tea. Biscuits!
jokes.setup: Philip trying to maintain civilized tea time
jokes.punchline: Jazz enthusiastically embracing upper-class traditions
jokes.explanation: Simple fish-out-of-water humor with Jazz in fancy setting

jokes.text: I thought this was sterling. It's got that certain weight to it.
jokes.setup: Jazz handling fancy tea service
jokes.punchline: His amateur assessment of expensive silver
jokes.explanation: Mild class humor but not particularly sharp

jokes.text: You drop-kicked a human being across our front lawn.
jokes.setup: Philip claims he's calm and in control
jokes.punchline: Vivian describes his violent ejection of Jazz
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy description with specific, funny imagery

jokes.text: It felt good, damn it.
jokes.setup: Vivian criticizes Philip's violence
jokes.punchline: Philip admits he enjoyed it
jokes.explanation: Philip's rare moment of honest aggression - breaking character amusingly

jokes.text: I'm back in the saddle again.
jokes.setup: After violently ejecting Jazz
jokes.punchline: Philip uses cowboy metaphor for regaining control
jokes.explanation: Philip's satisfaction with violent solution, western metaphor is mild

jokes.text: You know, I think living down the street from the Reagans has had a very bad effect on you.
jokes.setup: Philip's violent reaction to Jazz
jokes.punchline: Vivian blames proximity to conservative political figures
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor connecting Philip's authoritarianism to famous neighbors

jokes.text: I hope this isn't going to be one of those Theo moments.
jokes.setup: Philip preparing for serious family conversation
jokes.punchline: Will references 'Cosby Show' serious moment trope
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about sitcom tropes with specific 'Cosby Show' reference

jokes.text: You must have caught her on one of her rare free nights.
jokes.setup: Philip mentions calling Madame Chatchka for makeup lesson
jokes.punchline: Will references her supposed busy romantic life
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier violin teacher joke

jokes.text: These kids today have no attention span. I blame television.
jokes.setup: Ashley wants to quit all her activities
jokes.punchline: Carlton blames TV while being on a TV show
jokes.explanation: Meta-ironic moment with Carlton unknowingly criticizing his own medium
